fields.Text: The renal CT phase with highest sensitivity for detecting RCC is the {{c1::nephrographic phase}}.
fields.Extra:
Q1. Correct; difficulty: hard. The nephrographic phase of imaging is the most sensitive in the detection of renal cell cancer, especially when the primary lesion is small.




Open review Q1

fields.Text:
Intercostal angiography with visible artery of Adamkiewicz. Feared complication?

{{c1::Paralysis/paraplegia}}
fields.Extra:
Q2. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: The Artery of Adamkiewicz is the dominant spinal cord blood supply, typically arising from left intercostal arteries and notable for its characteristic hairpin turn on angiography. Inadvertent injury during endovascular procedures risks catastrophic spinal cord ischemia and paraplegia.





Open review Q2

fields.Text: Injury to the artery of Adamkiewicz during intercostal/bronchial angiography can cause {{c1::spinal cord ischemia and paralysis/paraplegia}}.
fields.Extra:
Q2. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: The Artery of Adamkiewicz is the dominant spinal cord blood supply, typically arising from left intercostal arteries and notable for its characteristic hairpin turn on angiography. Inadvertent injury during endovascular procedures risks catastrophic spinal cord ischemia and paraplegia.






Open review Q2

fields.Text:
Nuclear medicine study using Y-90-type therapy radionuclide. Primary decay mode?

{{c1::Beta minus}}
fields.Extra:
Q3. Correct; difficulty: hard. The study shown is a post-therapy I-131 scan for the treatment of metastatic thyroid cancer.





Open review Q3

fields.Text: Y-90 and several therapeutic radionuclides primarily decay by {{c1::beta-minus emission}}.
fields.Extra:
Q3. Correct; difficulty: hard. The study shown is a post-therapy I-131 scan for the treatment of metastatic thyroid cancer.

fields.Text: An outpatient MRI parking lot is {{c1::Zone I}} under ACR MRI safety criteria.
fields.Extra:
Q4. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: The American College of Radiology guidelines define the MRI parking lot as Zone I, which is the area freely accessible to the general public and poses no MRI-related safety risks.

fields.Text:
MRI with molar tooth sign and vermian hypoplasia. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Joubert syndrome}}

1/4
fields.Extra:
Q5. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Molar tooth sign combined with vermian hypoplasia represents the pathognomonic neuroimaging constellation that defines Joubert syndrome.







Open review Q5

fields.Text: Joubert syndrome is defined by {{c1::molar tooth sign with vermian hypoplasia}}.
fields.Extra:

fields.Text: Renal medullary carcinoma occurs almost exclusively in young patients with {{c1::sickle cell trait}}.
fields.Extra:
Q6. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Renal medullary carcinoma occurs almost exclusively in young patients with sickle cell trait. The tumor shows infiltrative growth pattern and central medullary location. Caliectasis is very commonly present. The prognosis is uniformly poor with aggressive metastatic behavior.




Open review Q6

fields.Text: After iodinated CT contrast, breastfeeding requires {{c1::no mandatory interruption}}.
fields.Extra:
Q7. Correct; difficulty: easy. Guidelines from the ACR (see reference) recommend no mandatory time period for breast-feeding mothers to resume feeds following the administration of iodinated contrast.




Open review Q7

fields.Text: The parotid space contains {{c1::periglandular and intraglandular lymph nodes}} that can enlarge from metastasis or lymphoma.
fields.Extra:
Q8. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: The parotid space contains 10 to 20 periglandular and intraglandular lymph nodes that can represent parotid neoplasms when enlarged, requiring evaluation for metastatic disease or lymphoma.

{{c1::Vesicular stage}}

fields.Text:
Chest radiograph with visible pleural line and absent peripheral lung markings. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Pneumothorax}}
fields.Extra:
Q10. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Pneumothorax, refers to the presence of gas (often air) in the pleural space. A pneumothorax is usually appreciated readily on erect chest radiographs, which reveal a visible visceral pleural edge seen as a very thin, sharp white line, with an absence of lung markings peripheral to this line. If there is a shift in the mediastinum, a tension pneumothorax is present and should be decompressed rapidly using needle aspiration.





Open review Q10

fields.Text: Pneumothorax on upright chest radiograph shows a {{c1::thin visceral pleural line with absent peripheral lung markings}}.
fields.Extra:
Q10. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Pneumothorax, refers to the presence of gas (often air) in the pleural space. A pneumothorax is usually appreciated readily on erect chest radiographs, which reveal a visible visceral pleural edge seen as a very thin, sharp white line, with an absence of lung markings peripheral to this line. If there is a shift in the mediastinum, a tension pneumothorax is present and should be decompressed rapidly using needle aspiration.

fields.Text:
Inversion recovery curve with null point around 500 ms. Appropriate TI?

{{c1::500 ms}}

fields.Text: Patient questions and concerns before a procedure are addressed through {{c1::informed consent}}.
fields.Extra:
Q20. Correct; difficulty: easy. The ACR-SIR Practice Parameter on Informed Consent for Image-Guided Procedures notes that “informed consent is a process and not the simple act of signing a formal document.” Informed consent is the process of explaining and obtaining permission for a procedure that will be performed on a patient.

fields.Text:
V/Q scan with matched defects by 0.5-segment mismatch criteria. PE probability?

{{c1::Very low probability of PE}}

1/2
fields.Extra:
Q21. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: figure "Planar perfusion scintigrams (lower row) with abnormalities, partly matched with abnormalities on planar ventilation scintigrams (upper row) are presented in the picture. The case was assessed as PE negative according to 0.5 segment mismatch criteria, and as non-diagnostic when PIOPED criteria were used. Using PISA-PED criteria the≈study was assessed as PE positive. (ANT = anterior, POS = posterior, RPO = right posterior oblique, LPO = left posterior oblique). After 12 month follow up the case was closed as PE negative. https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4078029/figure/f1-rado-48-02-113/":

fields.Text: The annual total effective occupational dose limit for a radiation worker is {{c1::50 mSv}}.
fields.Extra:
Q24. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Work with sources of ionizing radiation is regulated so that doses received by individuals do not exceed the applicable limit, with doses maintained as low as reasonably achievable (ALARA). Annual limit on intake (ALI) refers to the derived limit for radioactive material taken into the body of an adult worker by inhalation or ingestion in a year. The total effective occupational dose limit for a radiation worker is 50 mSv or 5 rem.

Q39. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Lobar holoprosencephaly shows minimal fusion of rostral-inferior frontal lobes with absent septum pellucidum. Posterior corpus callosum is formed while anterior portion may be absent. Variable basal ganglia and thalamic fusion occurs. Minimal fusion can make differentiation from other midline malformations challenging.

Q47. Correct; difficulty: not listed. The patient has a clearly visible left lower quadrant ostomy (red arrow).

fields.Text: The ACR/MQSA average glandular dose limit for one CC mammographic view with grid is {{c1::3 mGy}}.
fields.Extra:
Q48. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The average glandular dose delivered by a single craniocaudal view of a 4.2 cm compressed breast consisting of 50% glandular and 50% adipose tissue must not exceed 0.3 rad (3 milligray), although it is generally much lower.




Open review Q48

fields.Text: Opioid oversedation with respiratory depression after fentanyl is treated with {{c1::naloxone}}.
fields.Extra:
Q49. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Opioid overdose is characterized by suppression of breathing, deep sedation, hypotension, and nausea/vomiting. Naloxone is the reversal agent of choice.




Open review Q49

fields.Text:
Postmenopausal bleeding with uterine MRI mass. Diagnosis?

{{c1::Endometrial cancer}}
fields.Extra:
Q50. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Endometrial cancer demonstrates intermediate T2 signal intensity (relative to hyperintense normal endometrium), restricted diffusion, and hypoenhancement relative to myometrium.

fields.Text: Post-traumatic pneumatocele and pulmonary contusion can occur after {{c1::blunt chest trauma}}.

fields.Text:
MRI with Dawson-finger-type demyelinating lesions. Expected CSF finding?

{{c1::Oligoclonal bands}}

1/2
fields.Extra:
Q52. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Multiple sclerosis shows ovoid T2 FLAIR hyperintense periventricular lesions oriented perpendicular to ventricles (Dawson's fingers) with dissemination in space involving periventricular, juxtacortical, infratentorial, and spinal cord regions. Oligoclonal bands in cerebrospinal fluid support the diagnosis.






Open review Q52

fields.Text: Multiple sclerosis is supported by CSF {{c1::oligoclonal bands}}.
fields.Extra:

Oligoclonal bands in cerebrospinal fluid support the diagnosis.

fields.Text:
Scoliosis with plexiform neurofibromas. Underlying cause?

{{c1::Neurofibromatosis type 1}}
fields.Extra:
Q53. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Thoracic scoliosis with plexiform neurofibromas suggests neurofibromatosis type 1, where plexiform neurofibromas show heterogeneous high T2 signal with central low signal and carry malignant transformation risk.

fields.Text: Anterior shoulder dislocation can be associated with {{c1::greater tuberosity fracture}}.

fields.Text: Complete posterior acoustic shadowing is caused by {{c1::attenuation}} of the ultrasound beam.
fields.Extra:
Q55. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Complete acoustic shadowing occurs when structures totally attenuate ultrasound energy. Attenuation is the key concept because complete energy loss prevents sound transmission to deeper tissues. This eliminates echo formation and creates the anechoic shadow.






Open review Q55
